channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ‘It’s going to be painful for a lot of people’: Software engineers could go extinct this year, says Claude Code creator
item.description: Boris Cherny says Claude Code has been writing all of his code since November.

item.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says sucking up to your boss won’t earn their trust
item.description: Want to earn the trust of your boss and peers? Sucking up to them won’t do the trick, says Amazon’s CEO Andy Jassy.

item.title: The AI leadership reckoning is here
item.description: Your CEO is in danger and generational change is afoot.

item.title: It just got easier for your company to hire immigrant tech workers
item.description: As President Trump has cracked down on all kinds of immigration, tech workers and students have been caught in the crosshairs. In a bid to curb use of...

item.title: The Ultra-Rich Are Fleeing High‑Tax Countries. Here’s Where They’re Moving
item.description: There’s a few countries attracting the lion’s share.

item.title: Why Reputation Leadership Is Now a Core Business Function
item.description: Leaders who treat trust and credibility as strategic assets will scale sustainably.

item.title: Anthropic claims 3 Chinese companies ripped it off, using its AI tools to train their models: ‘How the turn tables’
item.description: Anthropic, which settled a copyright claim by thousands of authors for $1.5 billion months ago, claims the Chinese firms used "distillation" technique...

item.title: TED’s Audacious Project raises $1 billion in 2 days to fund initiatives for these winning nonprofits
item.description: Last October, 35 major donor families, calling their collaborative The Audacious Project , gathered in California and committed $1.03 billion to more ...

item.title: Successful Companies Stay Close to Their Customers
item.description: In a culture obsessed with speed and scale, the real advantage comes from listening to customers and prioritizing their trust.

item.title: The future of fashion
item.description: You’re invited to a holiday party with a dress code—cocktail attire. Instead of panic-scrolling through a bunch of dresses that look great on someone ...

item.title: Rare disease isn’t rare—it’s a healthcare blind spot
item.description: In medicine, “rare” is often used to describe conditions that affect relatively few people. But when you work in healthcare long enough—especially at ...

item.title: Can Kim Kardashian help an energy drink break through a crowded market?
item.description: Throughout Kim Kardashian’s two-decade career in the public eye, the reality TV star’s entrepreneurial endeavors have included shapewear clothing bran...

item.title: They Built a Meal Delivery Unicorn. Now They Have $75 Million to Invest in the Next Big Thing
item.description: The co-founders of Cutting Horse have their sights set on supplements, pet food, AI software, and other consumer products

item.title: Steve Jobs adopted a no ‘bozos’ policy and said the best managers are those who never wanted the job—here are his 3 best management tips
item.description: Hiring only for “professional management” doesn’t work, Steve Jobs said.

item.title: A Divorce Lawyer for the Superrich Says This 1 Word Will Make You a Much Better Negotiator
item.description: A divorce lawyer for super high net worth individuals uses this word to unlock even the most emotional and adversarial negotiations.

item.title: Economic calendar: Home-price and consumer-confidence data incoming
FedEx lawsuit vs. Trump administration kicks off war over tariff refunds
Claude developer Anthropic’s much-anticipated AI event under way — live updates
Consumers are less pessimistic about the U.S. economy than previously thought
